The 118 responds to calls dealing with parents and their children, including a disastrous birthday party thrown by a mommy blogger; Athena and Michael talk to May about her past suicide attempt; Chimney and Maddie adjust to life with their newborn.

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell is guest host with comedian Ivo Graham and journalist Isabel Hardman joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as guest panellists to delve into the news.

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's New World Order
programme.desc: In a time where Donald Trump can be president, who better to dissect the news than Frankie Boyle? For this edition, Frankie is joined by Sara Pascoe and Katherine Ryan.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig looks at a range of unrelated 'U' topics with Tom Allen, Chris McCausland, Lou Sanders and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: The unsparing panel show that puts the mockers on the world's headline-makers. This 'best of' end-of-series special also features riotous new material and daft outtakes.

programme.title: Would I Lie To You?
programme.desc: Star names turn 'true detective' in the mirthful fact-or-fiction panel show. Jo Brand, Roisin Conaty, Paul Foot and Ray Mears join Lee Mack, David Mitchell and Rob Brydon.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts an 'upsadaisy' episode and is joined by Justin Moorhouse, Emmanuel Sonubi, Holly Walsh and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Live at the Moth Club
programme.desc: Ellen has to move the green room downstairs so it's accessible for headliner Tim Renkow, but George hasn't got the memo and has given the space to a bingo club.

programme.title: Live at the Moth Club
programme.desc: An estate agent tells George that selling up would make him enormously wealthy - it looks like the writing's on the wall, but can the gang do anything about it.

programme.title: Special Ops: Crime Squad UK
programme.desc: Surrey police investigate a drug-dealing gang, farmers in Northamptonshire are targeted by a gang in the countryside, and a crime squad in Kent investigate residential burglaries.

programme.title: Special Ops: Crime Squad UK
programme.desc: Special police task forces tackle a hit-and-run in Lancashire which turns out to be a deliberate attack on a single mum, and they also tackle a drug-dealing family dynasty in Bury.

programme.desc: The Ghan's maintenance team must race to ensure this iconic train departs the next day, and a routine job on a Queensland Rail commuter train hits a snag.

programme.desc: On the Cesme Peninsula, Rick Stein enjoys creamy Armola cheese, Urla's famous red wine, and the ultimate Turkish breakfast.

programme.title: Border Force America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: 120 kilograms of cocaine are discovered in the axles of a lowboy trailer, and two men are caught attempting to smuggle a rooster for cock-fighting.

programme.title: Border Force America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Packages of cocaine are found concealed in the chassis of a bus, officers receive a tip-off about a vehicle loaded with cartel cash, and a magician comes under scrutiny.

programme.title: How Britain Worked
programme.desc: Guy Martin and a team from the Black Country Living Museum restore a Newcomen engine, an 18th-century steam-powered device used principally to pump water out of mines.

programme.desc: Gear up for more fun with the motor-mad motormouths. Jeremy builds a car that's even smaller than the famous Peel P50, and Damian Lewis is the Star in the Reasonably Priced Car.
